Details: In presence of copper ions and L- ascorbic acid, hydrogen peroxide effectively (45 to 90 percent) oxidizes carbofuran after 10 minutes of reaction time.  However, low doses of hydrogen peroxide alone (0.0034 mg/L) are not effective for the removal of carbofuran. Based on the results of one study, only 5 to 10 percent removal was achieved after 125 to 245 hours using hydrogen peroxide in the absence of sunlight.
